name: Wysa
stated_pop: General population (adults; child version available); employees via employer programs
note: Transdiagnostic AI-CBT chatbot; covers anxiety, depression, sleep, chronic pain, exam stress, low energy, loneliness, trauma, LGBTQ+. NHS-accelerator listed.
elevator_pitch: AI-driven mental-wellbeing chat companion that walks users through CBT-based exercises (and optional human therapist add-on) for anxiety, low mood, sleep, chronic pain, and stress.
usage_adoption: 5M+ self-reported users globally; >45 published studies cite improvement on PHQ-9 and GAD-7; NHS-listed (NHS Innovation Accelerator); deployed in employer wellbeing programs and an NHS-Talking-Therapies pilot.
cbt_location: CBT shows up inside the chat as discrete tool-cards triggered by conversation: 'reframe a thought', 'cognitive restructuring', 'gratitude reflection', 'thought reframer', 'sleep meditation', 'plan a worry-free day', etc. Premium tier adds a chat-based CBT-trained human coach.
cbt_structure: Open-ended chat with branching tool packs rather than linear sessions; specialised tracks exist (e.g., chronic-pain CBT-based pack used in JMIR study). Self-paced 5–15-min daily check-ins.
cbt_ui_examples: Penguin avatar, conversational chat bubbles with quick-reply chips. Thought-record screens prompt the user to type the thought, identify the cognitive distortion from a list, then write a balanced alternative.
all_features: AI chatbot [CBT]; thought reframer [CBT]; cognitive-restructuring exercises [CBT]; behavioural activation prompts [CBT]; problem-solving worksheets [CBT]; mood tracker; sleep stories; meditations; breathing exercises; gratitude journal; physical-activity prompts; in-app SOS routing; optional human coach (premium) [CBT].
cbt_concepts: Cognitive distortions, thought records, cognitive restructuring/reframing, behavioural activation, problem-solving, relaxation/arousal reduction, psychoeducation, self-monitoring.
cbt_tools: Conversational chatbot (primary delivery), thought-record screens, mood logs, gratitude journal, breathing exercises, guided meditations, optional human-coach chat.
daily_content: Daily AI check-ins with mood + reason; suggested 'tools of the day' rotated based on conversation.
psychoeducation: Brief in-chat explainers on CBT concepts (e.g., what cognitive distortions are, why behavioural activation works), short audio/video clips, FAQs in help centre.
standardized_measures: PHQ-9 (depression) and GAD-7 (anxiety) administered in-app at intake and follow-up; PROMIS scales reported in some studies.
other_assessments: Brief in-chat self-rating prompts (mood 1–10, sleep quality, pain intensity) feed into tool recommendations.
cbt_degree: Core — CBT is the primary therapeutic frame. Mindfulness and positive-psychology content are layered alongside but the active ingredient (per RCT evidence) is the CBT toolset.

name: SilverCloud (Space from Anxiety & Depression / Unified Protocol)
stated_pop: Adults; perinatal; cancer survivors (research RCTs); university students
note: Suite of iCBT programs incl. Unified-Protocol transdiagnostic intervention for mixed anxiety/depression; supported and self-guided variants.
elevator_pitch: Clinically-validated supported-iCBT platform with disorder-specific 'Space from…' programs (Anxiety, Depression, Stress, Sleep, Health Anxiety, Chronic Pain, Mixed) used by NHS, HSE Ireland, and US health systems.
app_store_url: Unknown (delivered primarily as web app; mobile app exists per individual deployment partners)
usage_adoption: Deployed across NHS Talking Therapies, HSE Ireland, US health systems, universities; cited in dozens of peer-reviewed evaluations; up to 80% of users show improvement; used in cancer-survivor RCTs.
cbt_location: Modular structured CBT programs branded 'Space from…'. Each program is split into ~10 modules covering psychoeducation → behavioural activation → cognitive restructuring → exposure → relapse prevention.
cbt_structure: 10 modules per program: Getting Started; Understanding Depression & Anxiety; Noticing Feelings; Boosting Behaviour; Spotting Thoughts; Challenging Thoughts; Managing Worry; Core Beliefs (coach-unlocked); Facing Your Fears (coach-unlocked); Bringing It All Together. ~7-week course with weekly coach review.
cbt_ui_examples: Web/mobile-responsive UI with module-cards, embedded videos, quizzes, drag-and-drop activities, downloadable worksheets, in-line thought-record forms, mood/activity tracker, journal.
all_features: Modular CBT lessons [CBT]; thought-record / cognitive-restructuring tools [CBT]; behavioural-activation activity scheduler [CBT]; exposure / fear-ladder tool [CBT]; mood tracker; journal; videos; quizzes; coach messaging [CBT support]; downloadable PDF worksheets [CBT]; goal-setting; relaxation/mindfulness audio.
cbt_concepts: Psychoeducation, behavioural activation, cognitive restructuring, core-belief work, graded exposure, problem-solving, relapse prevention; Unified-Protocol transdiagnostic variant available.
cbt_tools: Interactive web modules, thought records, activity diaries, fear ladders, journal, downloadable PDFs, coach messaging.
daily_content: Weekly module assignment (1 module/week); daily homework activities (mood log, activity schedule, thought record).
psychoeducation: Substantial: each module opens with multimedia psychoeducation (videos, animations, written content) on the CBT model and on the targeted condition.
standardized_measures: PHQ-9, GAD-7, WHO-5 administered at baseline, mid-program and post; results visualised in a progress dashboard for user and coach.
other_assessments: WSAS (Work & Social Adjustment Scale) in some deployments; condition-specific measures (e.g., Insomnia Severity Index in Space from Sleep).
cbt_degree: Core — pure structured iCBT, including the Unified-Protocol transdiagnostic variant developed with Barlow et al.

name: Sanvello
stated_pop: Adults with mild-to-moderate anxiety, depression, stress
note: CBT + mindfulness + mood/sleep/activity tracking; community feature; therapy add-on available.
elevator_pitch: Self-help app combining CBT 'Guided Journeys', mood/health tracking, mindfulness, and optional coaching/therapy for adults with mild-to-moderate anxiety, depression, and stress.
usage_adoption: Ratings: 4.8★ (22.5K reviews) on App Store, 4.5★ (24.9K) on Google Play; covered by major US health plans (UnitedHealthcare); ~3 million members lifetime.
cbt_location: Self-paced 'Guided Journeys' built on CBT principles (e.g., 'Getting Started with CBT', 'Coping with Anxiety', 'Healthier Thinking'). Tools layered alongside mindfulness and peer features.
cbt_structure: Mix of short courses (5–10 lessons each) and bite-sized daily exercises; not strictly sequential; users can pick journeys.
cbt_ui_examples: Card-style home feed with daily check-in prompt, mood slider, list of next-up Journey lessons. Thought-distortion exercise asks user to identify distortion type from a list and write reframe.
all_features: CBT Guided Journeys [CBT]; thought-distortion identifier [CBT]; mood tracker; health tracker (sleep/caffeine/exercise); guided meditations; deep-breathing tool; goal setting [CBT homework]; peer-support community; coaching (premium); therapy (premium); progress charts; assessments.
cbt_concepts: Cognitive distortions (10-style list), thought records, behavioural activation (activity scheduling), goal setting, problem solving, relaxation.
cbt_tools: Mood logs, thought-record exercises, activity tracker, mindfulness/meditation library, peer-community posts.
daily_content: Daily mood check-in; daily 'Today' card surfaces a relevant CBT exercise or meditation.
psychoeducation: Short articles + video lessons inside Journeys explaining the CBT model, cognitive distortions, behavioural activation rationale.
standardized_measures: Self-assessment yields anxiety / stress / depression scores at intake and over time (Sanvello clinical assessment, derived from PHQ/GAD-style screening); results feed personalised exercise recommendations.
other_assessments: Mood-tracking time series; activity-frequency data used to surface Journeys.
cbt_degree: Mixed (CBT is primary, mindfulness is co-equal) — CBT is explicitly the principal framework but the app is openly hybrid with mindfulness, meditation, and peer support.

name: moodgym
stated_pop: Adults; emerging-adult/student cohorts
note: Long-running NHMRC-funded iCBT program; clinical-guideline-recommended.
elevator_pitch: Long-running self-guided web-based interactive CBT training for adults to prevent and reduce depression and anxiety; foundational Australian iCBT program from ANU.
usage_adoption: 1.3M+ registered users worldwide; cited in 2016 meta-analysis of 12 trials showing efficacy in adults; recommended in RANZCP clinical-practice guidelines.
cbt_location: Five sequential modules: Why You Feel the Way You Do; Changing the Way You Think; Changing 'Warpy' Thoughts; Knowing What Makes You Upset; Assertiveness & Interpersonal Skills.
cbt_structure: 5 modules + workbook; ~30–45 min/module; designed to be completed over 4–6 weeks; self-paced with no clinician.
cbt_ui_examples: Web pages with characters (Elle, Will, etc.) modelling thought-feeling links; embedded interactive quizzes, rating scales, and 'warpy thought' identification exercises.
all_features: 5 CBT modules [CBT]; assessment quizzes [CBT progress]; 'warpy thought' identifier [CBT]; relaxation downloads; workbook; mood-rating scales; interactive characters/scenarios.
cbt_concepts: Cognitive distortions ('warpy thoughts'), thought-record style reframing, ABC model, behavioural strategies, assertiveness, problem solving.
cbt_tools: Interactive web exercises, downloadable workbook, quizzes, audio relaxation tracks.
daily_content: No daily push; module-paced.
psychoeducation: Substantial — each module begins with character-driven psychoeducation explaining the cognitive model, common distortions, and CBT skills.
standardized_measures: Goldberg Depression / Anxiety scales used in study evaluations; in-product self-rating quizzes track progress; not a clinical-grade integration.
other_assessments: Built-in pre/post-module quizzes feed back to user.
cbt_degree: Core — pure CBT skills training; no mindfulness or other modality.

name: THIS WAY UP — Mixed Depression & Anxiety (Transdiagnostic Course)
stated_pop: Adults with mixed anxiety + depression; perinatal; chronic pain (separate course)
note: Clinician-prescribable iCBT clinic; transdiagnostic course alongside disorder-specific (OCD, social anxiety, health anxiety, insomnia, chronic pain) courses.
elevator_pitch: Clinician-prescribable iCBT clinic from St Vincent's / UNSW with 30+ disorder-specific and transdiagnostic CBT courses (anxiety, depression, mixed, OCD, PTSD, health anxiety, chronic pain, insomnia, perinatal).
app_store_url: Unknown (web platform; courses delivered via browser)
google_play_url: Unknown (web platform; courses delivered via browser)
usage_adoption: Used by 10,000s of Australian patients via clinician referral or direct purchase ($59); listed by healthdirect; multiple RCTs and pragmatic trials published.
cbt_location: Each course is a structured iCBT program with 4–6 'lessons'. Mixed Anxiety & Depression Course is the transdiagnostic flagship; Chronic Pain Course adds CBT-for-pain.
cbt_structure: Typical course: 6 lessons over 6–12 weeks, lesson-released sequentially every 1–2 weeks. Lesson = comic-style story + skills demo + downloadable lesson summary + homework worksheet.
cbt_ui_examples: Comic-strip illustration showing a patient applying skills; 'Do Something' homework page with interactive worksheets and reflection prompts; clinician dashboard for prescribers.
all_features: 6-lesson CBT courses [CBT]; lesson summaries (PDF) [CBT]; homework worksheets [CBT]; thought-record templates [CBT]; behavioural-experiment templates [CBT]; symptom tracker; clinician messaging (when prescribed).
cbt_concepts: Cognitive restructuring, behavioural activation, graded exposure, behavioural experiments, problem solving, relapse prevention; transdiagnostic 'unified' framing in the Mixed course.
cbt_tools: Online lessons, PDF lesson summaries, structured homework worksheets, symptom-rating tools.
daily_content: No daily push; weekly lesson + daily homework practice.
psychoeducation: Strong narrative-driven psychoeducation in every lesson; lesson summaries serve as standalone CBT primers.
standardized_measures: PHQ-9, GAD-7, K-10 administered at intake and after each lesson; clinician dashboard surfaces scores over time.
other_assessments: Course-specific symptom measures (e.g., insomnia ISI in Insomnia Course; PCS in Chronic Pain Course).
cbt_degree: Core — strict structured iCBT; family-level transdiagnostic framework explicitly used in Mixed course.

name: MindSpot Wellbeing Course (incl. Mood Mechanic)
stated_pop: Australians 18+; Mood Mechanic 18-25; Wellbeing Plus 65+; Indigenous Wellbeing Course
note: National free transdiagnostic iCBT clinic; Mood Mechanic course is the closest age-aligned (18-25) AYA-relevant course.
elevator_pitch: Australia's free national digital mental-health clinic offering therapist-supported transdiagnostic iCBT courses (Mood Mechanic 18-25; Wellbeing 26-65; Wellbeing Plus 65+; Indigenous Wellbeing).
usage_adoption: 100,000+ Australians registered since 2012; outcomes published in Psychiatric Services and JMIR; symptoms reduce by ~50% on average.
cbt_location: Each course = 5 weekly online 'lessons' delivered as illustrated story; transdiagnostic CBT for anxiety + depression with skills covering psychoeducation, cognitive therapy, behavioural activation, graded exposure, sleep, problem solving.
cbt_structure: 5 lessons over 8 weeks; therapist-supported (weekly call/email) or self-paced. Mood Mechanic is the AYA-targeted variant.
cbt_ui_examples: Illustrated story-style lessons, downloadable lesson summary PDF, homework worksheets, clinician messaging panel.
all_features: 5 transdiagnostic CBT lessons [CBT]; lesson summary PDFs [CBT]; homework worksheets [CBT]; thought-monitoring tool [CBT]; behavioural-activation scheduler [CBT]; graded-exposure tool [CBT]; sleep-management module [CBT]; therapist messaging.
cbt_concepts: Recognising the symptom cycle, challenging unhelpful thoughts, behavioural activation, graded exposure (overcoming avoidance), sleep training, problem solving, relapse prevention.
cbt_tools: Lessons, summaries, homework worksheets, thought-record templates, exposure hierarchies, sleep diaries.
daily_content: No daily push; weekly lesson with daily homework practice.
psychoeducation: Strong — story-driven psychoeducation about the cognitive-behavioural model embedded in each lesson.
standardized_measures: PHQ-9, GAD-7, K-10 used at registration, weekly during treatment, post-treatment, and 3-month follow-up.
other_assessments: PRIME-MD for self-harm screening; suicide-risk follow-up protocol.
cbt_degree: Core — transdiagnostic CBT explicitly framed at the 'Wellbeing' family level.

name: e-couch
stated_pop: Adults; modules for depression, anxiety, social anxiety, separation/divorce, bereavement
note: CBT + IPT + psychoeducation; bereavement and social-anxiety modules.
elevator_pitch: ANU/e-hub Health's free self-help website covering depression, anxiety/worry, social anxiety, divorce, and bereavement using CBT, IPT, and psychoeducation.
usage_adoption: Companion to moodgym; used by general public and in research RCTs (Christensen et al.); 3 published RCTs of Depression program; effect sizes comparable to moodgym.
cbt_location: Each of the 5 programs has an Information module and a Toolkit module; CBT lives inside the Toolkit (e.g., 'Behavioural Activation', 'Activity Scheduling', 'Thinking Tools').
cbt_structure: Self-paced; users can read the information module then work through any toolkit they choose; no fixed sequence.
cbt_ui_examples: Text-heavy web pages with downloadable worksheets, structured 'thinking tool' forms, mood diaries, problem-solving templates.
all_features: 5 condition-specific programs [CBT subset]; thinking-tool worksheet [CBT]; activity-scheduling worksheet [CBT]; problem-solving worksheet [CBT]; relaxation audio; mood diary; downloadable PDFs; IPT module [non-CBT].
cbt_concepts: Behavioural activation, cognitive restructuring, problem solving, relaxation; IPT (interpersonal therapy) added for depression and bereavement.
cbt_tools: Web modules, downloadable PDF worksheets (thinking tools, activity logs, mood diaries), audio relaxation files.
daily_content: No daily push.
psychoeducation: Strong — each program's Information module is essentially a long-form psychoeducation primer.
standardized_measures: Goldberg / CES-D used in published evaluations; in-product self-rating only.
other_assessments: Mood diary time series.
cbt_degree: Mixed — CBT and IPT both used; Depression and Bereavement programs explicitly combine the two.

name: Beating the Blues
stated_pop: Adults with mild-to-moderate depression and/or anxiety in primary care
note: Long-running NHS-deployed cCBT; 8-9 sessions, video vignettes; primary-care use.
elevator_pitch: Long-established UK NHS-deployed cCBT for primary-care patients with depression and/or anxiety; 8-session interactive multimedia program with patient-monitoring back-end.
usage_adoption: Used in NHS for >12 years; recommended by NICE (initial recommendation); deployed by IAPT/Talking Therapies services across UK; ~1M user sessions logged.
cbt_location: 8 sequential 50-minute sessions, each made up of 3–5 modules of 10–15 min covering CBT skills.
cbt_structure: Intro video + 8 weekly sessions; each session: psychoeducation video → interactive task → printable homework → end-of-session report.
cbt_ui_examples: Multimedia: animations, video vignettes of patients, voice-overs; interactive symptom tracker; goal-tracker; printable homework PDFs.
all_features: 8 CBT sessions [CBT]; identifying / challenging unhelpful thoughts [CBT]; core-belief work [CBT]; activity scheduling [CBT]; problem solving [CBT]; graded exposure [CBT]; sleep management [CBT]; relapse prevention [CBT]; risk assessment; symptom tracker; goal tracker; clinician monitoring portal.
cbt_concepts: Identifying treatment goals, challenging unhelpful thoughts, core-belief work, attributional styles, activity scheduling, problem solving, graded exposure, sleep management, relapse prevention.
cbt_tools: Interactive videos, thought-record exercises, activity logs, problem-solving worksheets, sleep diary, printable homework PDFs.
daily_content: Weekly session + daily homework tasks (e.g., activity log, thought record).
psychoeducation: Strong — every session opens with video psychoeducation and case vignettes.
standardized_measures: Configurable; PHQ-9, GAD-7, BDI-II commonly used; end-of-session reports include outcome measures.
other_assessments: Risk assessment screen with automated alerts when self-harm indicated.
cbt_degree: Core — pure structured CBT; no other modalities.

name: Catch It
stated_pop: General population; mood / anger / anxiety self-management
note: CBT mood diary (Catch It / Check It / Change It); free Apple/Google.
elevator_pitch: Free academic-built CBT mood diary from Universities of Liverpool and Manchester; teaches the Catch-It / Check-It / Change-It cycle for re-framing unhelpful thoughts.
usage_adoption: Free on both stores; published feasibility study showed 84% of user-generated content was CBT-consistent and statistically significant reductions in negative-mood intensity.
cbt_location: Three-section workflow: Catch It (record mood), Check It (re-frame), Change It (alternative response). Pure CBT thought-record-style structure.
cbt_structure: Open-ended diary; no sessions or levels; user logs as moods occur.
cbt_ui_examples: Form-style entry — pick mood, intensity (1–5), context, automatic thought, then prompts for alternative thought and helpful response.
all_features: Mood diary [CBT]; thought-record (3-step CBT cycle) [CBT]; re-framing prompts [CBT]; PIN protection; export.
cbt_concepts: Thought records, cognitive re-framing, ABC model.
cbt_tools: Mood log + thought-record entry form.
daily_content: User-driven (log when a mood occurs); no scheduled push.
psychoeducation: Minimal — short in-app description of the CBT model; primarily a tool, not a course.
standardized_measures: None integrated; not a screener.
other_assessments: User-rated mood-intensity score is the primary signal.
cbt_degree: Core — single-purpose CBT thought-record tool.

name: Youper
stated_pop: Adults with anxiety/depression; AYA studies in young adults
note: Stanford-validated AI CBT chatbot; emotion-regulation focus; PHQ-9 / GAD-7 outcomes.
elevator_pitch: AI conversational agent trained on CBT (and other empirically-supported therapies) for adults with anxiety and depression; Stanford-validated.
usage_adoption: 3M+ users; Google Play 3.9★ (49.4K reviews); Stanford-published validation (PHQ-9 d=0.46, GAD-7 d=0.57 at 2 weeks).
cbt_location: CBT shows up inside the chat as 'guided exercises' (e.g., Cognitive Restructuring; Worry Time; Behavioural Activation) initiated by the bot or chosen by user.
cbt_structure: Conversational; no fixed lesson sequence; daily check-in pattern with situationally-suggested CBT exercises.
cbt_ui_examples: Chat UI; private 'Thought Diary' screens with cognitive-distortion picker; mood-tracking emoji slider; SOS breathing exercise screen.
all_features: AI chat agent [CBT]; thought-diary (CBT thought record) [CBT]; mood tracker; voice-journaling (premium); meditations; SOS breathing; daily check-ins; AI-personalised CBT exercises [CBT]; library of micro-interventions; coaching content (premium).
cbt_concepts: Cognitive distortions, thought records, cognitive restructuring, behavioural activation, exposure (worry-time / postponement), problem solving.
cbt_tools: Chatbot, private thought diary, mood tracker, journaling, audio meditations.
daily_content: Daily AI check-in with mood + reasons; routine prompts to log emotion.
psychoeducation: In-chat micro-explanations of CBT concepts; brief written content; audio meditations.
standardized_measures: PHQ-9 and GAD-7 administered in-app at baseline and follow-ups (used in Stanford trial).
other_assessments: In-chat mood / emotion-intensity ratings.
cbt_degree: Core — explicitly CBT-first; mindfulness/meditation is supplementary.

name: Togetherall
stated_pop: Adults & students; member-led peer community + clinician-monitored self-care courses
note: Anonymous moderated peer community + GAD-7/PHQ-9 self-assessments + self-guided CBT-based wellness courses.
elevator_pitch: 24/7 anonymous online peer-support community for adults and students, with always-on clinician moderators ('Wall Guides'), validated screeners (PHQ-9/GAD-7), and short self-guided CBT-based wellness courses.
usage_adoption: 4.6M+ students at 450+ colleges/universities globally; commissioned by NHS, Veterans Affairs, US universities; multiple peer-reviewed engagement studies.
cbt_location: Self-care library hosts ~10–15 short CBT-informed courses (anxiety, low mood, sleep, anger) alongside non-CBT content; CBT not the primary offering.
cbt_structure: Self-paced courses (3–6 short modules each); peer community is the primary product.
cbt_ui_examples: Forum-like 'wall' with anonymous posts; clinician-moderated; course pages with readings + reflection prompts; assessment dashboard.
all_features: Anonymous peer community (forums, 'bricks'); 24/7 clinician moderation; validated screeners (PHQ-9, GAD-7); self-guided wellness courses (some CBT-informed [CBT]); journaling; one-to-one chat; resource library.
cbt_concepts: Light coverage: cognitive re-framing, behavioural activation, sleep hygiene appear in some courses.
cbt_tools: Online courses, journal, peer threads.
daily_content: Optional — community is always live; no enforced daily curriculum.
psychoeducation: Modest — courses provide condensed psychoeducation; primary value is peer connection.
standardized_measures: PHQ-9 and GAD-7 administered at registration and periodically.
other_assessments: Engagement metrics (posts, likes) used in evaluation studies.
cbt_degree: Mixed (CBT loosely referenced) — peer-support is the primary modality; CBT-informed courses are auxiliary content rather than a structured program.

name: Deprexis
stated_pop: Adults with depression (DiGA-prescribed)
note: Germany DiGA-reimbursed CBT-multimodal program; integrates ACT/mindfulness/positive psychology with CBT.
elevator_pitch: Highly tailored 10-module web-based depression program from Gaia AG; integrates CBT with ACT, mindfulness, IPT, and positive psychology; DiGA-prescribed in Germany.
usage_adoption: DiGA-reimbursed in Germany since 2020; 11+ RCTs published; effect size d≈0.51 vs control; used in psychotherapy adjunct trials (US VA RCT ongoing 2024).
cbt_location: CBT is the dominant module set: Behavioural Activation, Cognitive Modification (with thought records and distortions), Problem Solving — though the program also includes ACT/mindfulness, IPT, positive psychology, and dream-work.
cbt_structure: 10 modules (intro; behavioural activation; cognitive modification; lifestyle; acceptance & mindfulness; problem solving; childhood experiences; interpersonal skills; positive psychology; emotion-focused/dream-work). Each 10-60 min; min 6 modules to complete.
cbt_ui_examples: Simulated dialogue ('Broca' tailoring engine) — Q&A flow, examples, branching content; printable PDF worksheets and summaries; audio recordings; symptom-tracking line charts.
all_features: 10 modules [CBT subset]; behavioural-activation log [CBT]; cognitive-distortion identifier [CBT]; thought-record [CBT]; problem-solving worksheet [CBT]; ACT/mindfulness exercises [non-CBT]; printable PDFs [CBT]; audio; symptom tracker.
cbt_concepts: Behavioural activation, cognitive distortions, cognitive restructuring (bird's-eye-view; scientist's perspective), problem solving.
cbt_tools: Interactive web dialogue, worksheets/summaries (PDF), audio, symptom-tracking charts.
daily_content: Self-paced; modules typically 1–2/week.
psychoeducation: Strong — Q&A psychoeducation woven throughout; explicit explanations of cognitive distortions, BA rationale, etc.
standardized_measures: PHQ-9 / BDI-II in trials and DiGA evaluation; in-product symptom tracker.
other_assessments: User responses tailor content (Broca engine).
cbt_degree: Mixed (CBT primary) — explicitly multi-modal but CBT-anchored; the program author describes it as 'integrative'.

name: Velibra
stated_pop: Adults with GAD, panic disorder ± agoraphobia, social anxiety disorder
note: Anxiety-family transdiagnostic (covers 3 anxiety disorders); German DiGA-reimbursed.
elevator_pitch: DiGA-reimbursed German web-based unguided iCBT for adults with GAD, panic disorder, or social anxiety; 6 modules combining CBT skills with cognitive-bias modification (CBM-I) training.
usage_adoption: Reimbursed via DiGA in Germany; RCT in primary care across DE/AT/CH showed 28-45% remission across the three anxiety disorders post-treatment.
cbt_location: Six structured iCBT modules each followed by a CBM-I training session (interpretation-bias retraining).
cbt_structure: 6 modules; 90-day prescription window, 180-day recommended duration; unguided.
cbt_ui_examples: Web pages with text + image + audio + downloadable PDF summary/worksheet; daily SMS or email prompts; CBM-I training as ambiguous-scenario completion task.
all_features: 6 CBT modules [CBT]; CBM-I training sessions [CBT]; daily SMS/email reminders; PDF summaries/worksheets [CBT]; thought-record style exercises [CBT]; relaxation audio.
cbt_concepts: Psychoeducation, cognitive restructuring, interoceptive and situational exposure, problem-solving, relaxation; CBM-I (interpretation-bias retraining).
cbt_tools: Online modules, PDF worksheets, audio relaxation, CBM-I scenario tasks.
daily_content: Daily fully-automated SMS/email prompts during treatment.
psychoeducation: Strong — disorder-specific psychoeducation in each module.
standardized_measures: GAD-7 / PHQ-9 / SPIN / PDSS used in DiGA evaluation and trials.
other_assessments: In-product self-rating between modules.
cbt_degree: Core — pure CBT + an evidence-backed bias-modification add-on (still cognitive-mechanism-based).

name: SuperBetter
stated_pop: Adolescents & adults; resilience and mental-health goals
note: Game-based CBT + positive psychology. RCT evidence for depression and anxiety reductions.
elevator_pitch: Game-based mental-health-and-resilience app where users adopt a secret identity, complete quests, defeat 'bad guys', and check in with allies; integrates positive psychology with CBT principles.
usage_adoption: 1M+ players lifetime; UPenn and Harvard RCTs show significant reductions in depression and anxiety; largest effect size for depression in 2017 meta-analysis of 22 apps.
cbt_location: CBT principles surface as 'power-ups' (positive activities), 'bad guys' (negative thoughts/behaviours to fight), 'quests' (graduated challenges), and 'allies' (relatedness/social support).
cbt_structure: Library of 40+ solo challenges (each 7–30 day arcs) + 90+ host challenges; users adopt one challenge at a time; mostly self-paced, gameful.
cbt_ui_examples: Quest cards on home; 'epic win' progress bar; secret-identity dashboard; allies-list; bad-guys-defeated counter.
all_features: Quests [CBT-adjacent — graded exposure / behavioural activation]; power-ups [BA]; bad guys [cognitive defusion / behavioural targets]; allies (relatedness); secret identity (self-concept); progress charts; gamified achievements.
cbt_concepts: Behavioural activation (power-ups), cognitive re-framing (defeat-the-bad-guy), goal setting, graded exposure (quests), problem solving, social support; positive psychology (savouring, gratitude).
cbt_tools: In-app quest log, mood/energy/strength check-in, gameful achievements, allies network.
daily_content: Suggested daily quests/power-ups; gameful streak tracking.
psychoeducation: Light — gameful framing rather than explicit CBT lessons; embedded micro-tips link to underlying science.
standardized_measures: PHQ-9 / GAD-7 used in trials, optional in product.
other_assessments: Self-rated mood/energy/strength check-ins.
cbt_degree: Mixed — CBT and positive psychology are co-equal anchors; the gameful framing is stronger than explicit CBT framing.

name: BlueIce
vendor: Oxford Health NHS Foundation Trust / University of Bath
stated_pop: Adolescents 12-17 with self-harm and emotional dysregulation; CAMHS-prescribed
note: CBT + DBT toolbox for adolescent self-harm; mood diary, automatic safety-check routing, emergency contact escalation.
elevator_pitch: Prescribed UK CAMHS app for 12–17-year-olds who self-harm; CBT/DBT toolbox with mood diary, automatic mood-lifter routing, and emergency-contact escalation.
app_store_url: Unknown (prescribed app; access via clinician code)
usage_adoption: 2,520+ young people prescribed across multiple UK CAMHS sites (Bath, Oxfordshire, Cambridgeshire, Manchester, others); RCT (BASH study) and Phase-1 trials show ~73% of adolescents reported reduced self-harm at 12 weeks.
cbt_location: CBT shows up inside the personalised 'mood-lifter' toolbox triggered by low mood; toolbox combines CBT (e.g., thought challenging, behavioural activation) with DBT skills (distress tolerance) and mindfulness.
cbt_structure: No formal sessions; user-driven and triggered by low mood. App auto-routes to appropriate toolbox section.
cbt_ui_examples: Mood-rating slider (0–10) with auto-routing logic; personalised tool-cards; safety-net contact list; emergency-services button.
all_features: Mood diary [CBT/DBT mood log]; personalised mood-lifter toolbox (CBT + DBT + mindfulness + BA) [CBT subset]; thought-challenge tool [CBT]; behavioural-activation activities [CBT]; relaxation audio; emergency-contact routing; safety check.
cbt_concepts: Cognitive challenging of distressing thoughts, behavioural activation, problem solving, distress-tolerance (DBT), mindfulness.
cbt_tools: Mood diary, thought-challenge entries, audio relaxation/mindfulness, automatic routing logic.
daily_content: User-driven; no enforced daily content.
psychoeducation: Light — embedded short tips inside toolbox cards.
standardized_measures: RCSQ self-harm / mood ratings; PHQ-9 / GAD-7 / RCADS in evaluation studies.
other_assessments: 0–10 mood slider drives routing; safety-check questions before each potential self-harm moment.
cbt_degree: Mixed — explicitly CBT + DBT + mindfulness; CBT is a major component but not the sole framework.

name: COMPASS
stated_pop: Adults with long-term physical health conditions experiencing distress, anxiety, or depression
note: Therapist-supported digital CBT calibrated to LTC-related distress; tailored to the survivorship-adjacent population (chronic illness).
elevator_pitch: King's College London therapist-supported digital CBT for adults with long-term physical health conditions (diabetes, MS, etc.) experiencing distress, anxiety, or depression; 11 modules across 4 'compass quadrants'.
app_store_url: Unknown (web platform; deployed via NHS partners)
usage_adoption: RCT (Hudson et al., 2024) showed 89% clinically significant improvement in distress vs 45% control; real-world implementation in NHS LTC services; deployment with diabetes services ongoing.
cbt_location: 11 modules organised on a 'compass' diagram: Navigating Compass (mandatory) + 10 themed modules (managing uncertainty, managing symptoms, communication, sleep, fatigue, low mood, worry, etc.) tailored to LTC challenges.
cbt_structure: First module compulsory (interactive self-assessment + personalised formulation); remaining 10 modules selectable in any order over 10–12 weeks; 5 × 30-min therapist phone calls.
cbt_ui_examples: Compass diagram navigation; module pages with patient-stories, interactive self-assessment, goal-setting, and embedded thought-record / exposure tasks; PHQ-9/GAD-7 graphical timelines.
all_features: Self-assessment & formulation tool [CBT]; 11 CBT modules [CBT]; thought-record [CBT]; behavioural-experiment templates [CBT]; goal-setting [CBT]; activity scheduling [CBT]; sleep module; fatigue management; LTC-specific psychoeducation [survivorship-adjacent]; therapist messaging.
cbt_concepts: Cognitive restructuring, behavioural activation, behavioural experiments, problem solving, graded exposure, symptom-management, third-wave acceptance principles for chronic-condition adjustment.
cbt_tools: Online modules, structured worksheets, mood/symptom trackers, therapist-call notes.
daily_content: Module pace (~1/week); homework activities daily.
psychoeducation: Strong — every module pairs LTC-specific psychoeducation with patient stories and evidence-based CBT skills.
standardized_measures: PHQ-9 (depression), GAD-7 (anxiety), and a distress measure (DT/CDDS variant) graphically tracked.
other_assessments: Condition-specific measures used in deployment partnerships.
cbt_degree: Core — explicitly CBT, adapted to LTC context; this is the closest framework match for survivorship work.

name: Untire
stated_pop: Adult cancer patients & survivors with cancer-related fatigue
note: Cancer-specific transdiagnostic eHealth: CBT (My themes) + MBSR + physical activity + positive psychology.
elevator_pitch: Cancer-specific eHealth app for adult cancer patients and survivors managing cancer-related fatigue; CBT (My themes) + MBSR + physical-activity coaching + positive-psychology tips, in 25 countries.
usage_adoption: Available free in 25 countries; ranked one of ORCHA's top-5 cancer digital solutions globally (2021); RCT (Spahrkäs et al.) showed d=0.40 fatigue, d=0.32 QoL improvements; CE-marked medical device.
cbt_location: CBT lives in 'My themes' module — psychoeducation + thought records + behavioural-activation around fatigue, worry, sleep, and low mood. Mediation analysis (Spahrkäs 2022) shows fatigue catastrophising and depression are the active mechanisms.
cbt_structure: Four content pillars: My themes (CBT psychoeducation + reflective exercises); My exercises (mindfulness/MBSR audio); Physical activity (graded movement); Tips (positive psychology). Self-paced; weekly Quick-Scan check-in.
cbt_ui_examples: Theme cards (e.g., 'The fatigue trap', 'Worry'), reflective journal entries, audio mindfulness players, graded-activity charts, weekly Quick-Scan summary.
all_features: My themes — psychoeducation + journaling [CBT]; thought-record-style reflections [CBT]; behavioural-activation activity planner [CBT]; mindfulness audio [non-CBT]; physical-activity coaching; Quick-Scan weekly self-rating; positive-psychology tips.
cbt_concepts: Fatigue catastrophising, cognitive restructuring, behavioural activation (graded activity), problem solving, sleep hygiene; mediation evidence supports cognitive-mechanism activity.
cbt_tools: Reflective journal, activity planner, mood + energy logs, weekly Quick-Scan.
daily_content: Tips library; weekly Quick-Scan; user-paced theme work.
psychoeducation: Strong — cancer-fatigue-specific psychoeducation throughout My themes.
standardized_measures: Used in RCTs: CIS-fatigue, EORTC QLQ-C30 (fatigue + QoL); in-app Quick-Scan tracks fatigue, burden, happiness, satisfaction, energy leaks weekly.
other_assessments: Cognitive-mechanism mediators (fatigue catastrophising, depressive symptoms, mindfulness) measured in studies.
cbt_degree: Mixed — CBT for cancer-related fatigue is a primary component, but mindfulness-based stress-reduction and physical-activity coaching are co-equal pillars.

name: iCanThrive
stated_pop: Women cancer survivors within 5 years post-treatment (research-deployed)
note: Cancer-survivorship CBT/ACT mobile app with phone coaching; pilot evidence for depression and sleep.
elevator_pitch: 8-module mobile self-help intervention drawn from CBT and acceptance-based therapies for women cancer survivors within 5 years post-treatment.
usage_adoption: Pilot study (n=28) showed significant reductions in depression and sleep disruption; available on Google Play (US) following research study; current production status unclear (verify in human review).
cbt_location: Eight discrete CBT/ACT exercise modules: promoting a grateful outlook; challenging negative thoughts; living a value-driven life; problem solving; savouring positive experiences; reducing worry; breathing relaxation; identifying emotions.
cbt_structure: 8 self-paced exercise modules; supported by once-weekly phone-coaching call from a clinician.
cbt_ui_examples: Exercise screens with prompt + reflection field; 'Challenge a Thought' module asks user to type negative thought, identify distortion, write alternative; triangle-breathing video.
all_features: 8 CBT/ACT exercise modules [CBT subset]; thought-challenging module [CBT]; problem-solving module [CBT]; behavioural-activation (savouring) module; values-clarification (ACT) module [non-CBT]; breathing-relaxation video; phone coaching.
cbt_concepts: Cognitive restructuring (thought-challenging), problem solving, behavioural activation (savouring), worry-time, relaxation; ACT values clarification.
cbt_tools: Exercise modules, journaling fields, video, phone coaching.
daily_content: Designed for daily 5–15 min use during the 8-week pilot.
psychoeducation: Moderate — short cancer-survivor-tailored explainers within each module.
standardized_measures: CES-D (depression), PSQI (sleep), POMS (mood) used in pilot.
other_assessments: Brief in-app reflection ratings.
cbt_degree: Mixed — CBT and ACT are co-equal; explicitly described as drawing from 'CBT and acceptance-based therapies'.

name: iCanCope with Pain
stated_pop: Adolescents with chronic pain (juvenile arthritis, sickle cell, post-op, persistent pain)
note: Birnie/Stinson UCD exemplar; symptom trackers across pain/sleep/mood/function/energy; CBT coping toolbox + goal-setting + peer support + age-appropriate education.
elevator_pitch: Birnie/Stinson SickKids/UHN paediatric chronic-pain self-management app for 12–18-year-olds with chronic pain (juvenile arthritis, sickle cell, post-op pain, persistent pain); CBT coping toolbox + symptom tracking + goal setting + age-appropriate education.
usage_adoption: Pilot RCTs across multiple pain conditions; iCanCope POP (post-op) and JIA pilots published; iCanCope-SCD for sickle cell ongoing; access currently limited to research participants in some deployments.
cbt_location: CBT lives in the 'Coping Toolbox' (Component III) — pain-specific cognitive and behavioural strategies; complemented by symptom trackers, goal-setting, social support, and age-appropriate pain education.
cbt_structure: Five evidence-based components rather than sequential lessons: trackers; goal-setting; coping toolbox; social support; education.
cbt_ui_examples: Tap-to-rate trackers (pain, sleep, mood, function, energy on 0–10 scales); goal-setting form; coping toolbox cards (muscle relaxation, guided imagery, mindfulness, abdominal breathing, distraction, thought-challenging).
all_features: Symptom trackers (pain/sleep/mood/function/energy); goal-setting [CBT]; CBT coping toolbox [CBT]; muscle relaxation [CBT]; guided imagery [CBT]; mindfulness; abdominal breathing [CBT]; thought-challenging cards [CBT]; pain-education library; moderated peer-discussion 'question of the day' boards; reports/graphs.
cbt_concepts: Pain-specific cognitive restructuring, behavioural activation (graded activity, pacing), relaxation, attention diversion, problem solving, exposure to feared movement.
cbt_tools: Real-time trackers, goal-setting wizard, coping-tool cards, education library, peer discussion boards.
daily_content: Daily real-time symptom logging; question-of-the-day in social-support feed.
psychoeducation: Strong — pain-specific multimedia education (animations, videos, audio) on pain neuroscience and coping.
standardized_measures: PROMIS pain interference, NRS pain intensity, PSQI, mood scales in trials; built-in trackers visualise time-series.
other_assessments: Personalised goal achievement; functional-activity log.
cbt_degree: Core — CBT-for-pain is the active framework; closest UCD exemplar for AYA-adjacent chronic-illness work.

name: MindShift CBT
stated_pop: Youth and adults with anxiety (GAD, panic, social anxiety, phobias, health anxiety)
note: Single-symptom-domain (anxiety family). Spans GAD/panic/social/phobia/health-anxiety variants.
elevator_pitch: Free Anxiety-Canada-developed CBT toolkit for youth and adults with anxiety (GAD, panic, social, phobias, health anxiety); thought journal, fear ladder, coping cards, community forum.
usage_adoption: 770K+ Google Play downloads (Similarweb estimate, 2024); ~35K downloads/month; published feasibility RCT in psychiatric inpatients; widely used by Canadian mental-health services.
cbt_location: Multiple CBT toolsets accessed from a home menu — Quick Relief, Thought Journal, Coping Cards, Belief Experiment, Facing Fears (fear ladder), Chill Zone, Goals.
cbt_structure: Tool-first (not lesson-first); user opens whichever tool fits the moment. Anxiety-Canada also offers separate 'Healthy Habits' check-ins and structured 'Chill Out Tools'.
cbt_ui_examples: Thought-journal form (situation → thoughts → feelings → reframe), fear-ladder builder (write feared situation + rank), coping-cards library, breathing-bubble Chill Zone.
all_features: Thought Journal [CBT]; Coping Cards [CBT]; Belief Experiment [CBT]; Facing Fears (fear ladder/exposure) [CBT]; Chill Zone (breathing) [CBT]; Quick Relief; Healthy Habits; Goal-Setting [CBT]; Check-Ins; Community Forum [peer support].
cbt_concepts: Cognitive distortions, thought records, behavioural experiments (Belief Experiment), graded exposure (Fear Ladder), relaxation, goal setting.
cbt_tools: Thought-journal, fear-ladder builder, coping cards, belief-experiment template, breathing exercises, check-ins.
daily_content: Daily check-in option; otherwise tool-driven.
psychoeducation: Strong — anxiety-disorder-specific psychoeducation (GAD, social anxiety, panic, health anxiety, phobias) referenced from Anxiety-Canada library.
standardized_measures: Self-rated anxiety scale at intake/follow-up (not PHQ-9/GAD-7 by default).
other_assessments: Mood/anxiety tracker over time.
cbt_degree: Core — explicit CBT toolset; mindfulness present but secondary.

name: Daylight (DaylightRx)
stated_pop: Adults with worry / generalised anxiety disorder
note: Single-symptom (worry/GAD). FDA-cleared (DaylightRx).
elevator_pitch: FDA-cleared (DaylightRx) digital CBT for adults 22+ with worry / generalised anxiety disorder, delivered via voice-led interactive lessons on a phone.
usage_adoption: FDA-cleared (DaylightRx, 2024); deployed by US health systems; RCT (Carl et al.) showed 71% remission of GAD by week 10 (vs 33% control); Big Health is enterprise-deployed.
cbt_location: CBT delivered as structured lessons on applied relaxation, stimulus control, cognitive restructuring, and exposure to worry — explicitly framed as CBT for GAD.
cbt_structure: Daily lessons (~10 min each) over weeks; audio-led, voice-actor format; sequential, not branching.
cbt_ui_examples: Audio-first lesson player with voice-over guide; cards prompting written reflections; symptom-tracker dashboards; reminders.
all_features: Voice-led CBT lessons [CBT]; applied-relaxation training [CBT]; stimulus-control exercises [CBT]; cognitive-restructuring exercises [CBT]; exposure-to-worry tasks [CBT]; daily practice prompts; progress charts; GAD-7 check-ins.
cbt_concepts: Applied relaxation, stimulus control (worry-time / postponement), cognitive restructuring, imaginal exposure to worry.
cbt_tools: Audio lessons, structured worksheets, in-app reflection prompts.
daily_content: Yes — designed for ~10 min/day across the program window.
psychoeducation: Strong — every lesson is framed psychoeducationally before delivering the skill.
standardized_measures: GAD-7 is the primary outcome and built-in tracker; PHQ-9 in some deployments.
other_assessments: Worry-frequency self-ratings.
cbt_degree: Core — pure CBT for GAD.

name: Sleepio (SleepioRx)
stated_pop: Adults with insomnia disorder; secondary anxiety/depression effects in evidence base
note: Primarily single-target (insomnia) but RCT evidence for secondary anxiety, depression, fatigue improvement. NICE-recommended.
elevator_pitch: NICE-recommended, FDA-cleared (SleepioRx) digital CBT-I for adults with insomnia disorder; 6 weekly sessions led by an animated 'Prof' avatar based on Prof Colin Espie's CBT-I protocol.
google_play_url: Unknown (UK / US enterprise primary; mobile available via partner deployments)
usage_adoption: NICE-recommended (MTG70); FDA-cleared (SleepioRx); >12 RCTs; 76% achieve clinical improvement on insomnia; large UK NHS deployment.
cbt_location: Pure CBT-I: sleep-restriction, stimulus-control, cognitive restructuring of sleep-related thoughts, sleep hygiene.
cbt_structure: 6 weekly 20-min sessions with 'The Prof' avatar; each session unlocks 1 week after the previous; daily sleep-diary completion between sessions.
cbt_ui_examples: 'The Prof' animated character delivers each session as a guided dialogue; sleep-diary input form; 'Sleep school' library articles; community discussion board.
all_features: 6 CBT-I sessions [CBT]; daily sleep diary [CBT]; sleep-restriction calculator [CBT]; stimulus-control protocol [CBT]; cognitive-restructuring of sleep beliefs [CBT]; sleep-hygiene library; community board; library of articles.
cbt_concepts: Sleep restriction, stimulus control, cognitive restructuring of dysfunctional sleep beliefs, paradoxical intention, sleep hygiene, racing-mind techniques.
cbt_tools: Animated 'Prof' sessions, daily sleep diary, schedule calculator, sleep-thought journal.
daily_content: Daily sleep-diary entry; weekly session.
psychoeducation: Strong — embedded sleep-science psychoeducation across each session and the 'Sleep School' library.
standardized_measures: ISI (Insomnia Severity Index) primary; SCI (Sleep Condition Indicator); PHQ-9 / GAD-7 used in some deployments.
other_assessments: Sleep-diary-derived metrics (sleep efficiency, total sleep time, latency).
cbt_degree: Core — gold-standard CBT-I; nothing non-CBT in the active protocol.

name: BRAVE-ONLINE
stated_pop: Children 8-12 and teenagers 12-17 with anxiety; parent versions
note: Single-symptom (anxiety). Long-running paediatric/adolescent iCBT.
elevator_pitch: Free Australian University-of-Queensland online CBT program for children 8-12, teenagers 12-17, and their parents to manage anxiety; 10 sessions + 2 booster sessions.
app_store_url: Unknown (web platform, not a mobile app)
usage_adoption: Running for >13 years; deployed Australia-wide; multiple RCTs (Spence et al., March et al.) show significant reduction in anxiety diagnoses sustained at 12-month follow-up; large-scale dissemination study with 6,500+ users.
cbt_location: CBT skills delivered across 10 weekly sessions: relaxation, identification of emotions/thoughts, positive self-talk, coping skills, problem solving, graded exposure.
cbt_structure: 10 sessions, 30-60 min each, weekly; +2 booster sessions; parallel parent program with 6 sessions.
cbt_ui_examples: Web-based interactive sessions with cartoons, examples, character-led narrative; downloadable Resource Folder PDFs (worksheets, fact sheets, home-practice sheets).
all_features: 10 CBT sessions [CBT]; relaxation training [CBT]; thought-identification exercises [CBT]; positive self-talk training [CBT]; coping-skills modules [CBT]; problem-solving worksheet [CBT]; graded-exposure stepladder [CBT]; downloadable worksheets [CBT]; parent program; booster sessions.
cbt_concepts: Relaxation, emotion/thought identification, positive self-talk, cognitive restructuring, coping-skill development, problem solving, graded exposure (stepladder).
cbt_tools: Online sessions, downloadable PDF worksheets (challenges, fact sheets, home practice).
daily_content: Weekly session + daily home-practice exercises.
psychoeducation: Strong — narrative-driven psychoeducation throughout.
standardized_measures: SCAS / RCADS in studies; in-product self-rating between sessions.
other_assessments: Therapist/parent-rated anxiety measures in research deployments.
cbt_degree: Core — pure youth-anxiety CBT.

name: Lumi Nova: Tales of Courage
stated_pop: Children 7-12 with mild-to-moderate anxiety (GAD, social, separation, panic, phobias)
note: Single-symptom (anxiety). Class-1 SaMD; NICE-recommended; immersive game-based exposure CBT.
elevator_pitch: NICE-recommended Class-1 SaMD therapeutic game for children 7-12 with mild-to-moderate anxiety; immersive intergalactic adventure that delivers exposure-based CBT.
usage_adoption: NICE-recommended; commissioned by NHS (Oxleas, Bexley/Greenwich); peer-reviewed evaluation studies (Lockwood et al.) show reductions in anxiety across all subdomains in 8 weeks.
cbt_location: Exposure-CBT is the active ingredient — child sets a worry-related goal, builds a graded ladder of exposure 'Missions' (challenges), and reflects on each completion in-game.
cbt_structure: 8 game levels; each Mission broken into 3 smaller goals; 14 anxiety-related goals available (social, separation, specific phobias).
cbt_ui_examples: Space adventure game UI; character creation; planet exploration; mission-card with before/after reflection prompts; treasure/achievement collection.
all_features: Game-based exposure CBT [CBT]; mission/goal builder (graded ladder) [CBT]; before/after reflection prompts [CBT]; psychoeducation cut-scenes [CBT]; gamification (treasure, planets, levels); auto-conclude (30-40 min limits screen time); parent dashboard.
cbt_concepts: Graded exposure, behavioural experiments, psychoeducation, self-monitoring.
cbt_tools: In-game challenges (exposure tasks), reflection journal, parent dashboard, psychoeducation animations.
daily_content: Designed for ~30-40 min/day during the 6-8-week intervention.
psychoeducation: Strong — animated psychoeducation cut-scenes about anxiety and avoidance.
standardized_measures: Goal-Based Outcomes (GBO), self-rated worry/distress; RCADS/SCAS in evaluation.
other_assessments: In-game progress data.
cbt_degree: Core — exposure-CBT is the explicit active ingredient.

name: CBT-i Coach
vendor: U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs (National Center for PTSD) / DoD / Stanford
stated_pop: Adults engaged in CBT-I with a clinician; PTSD-related insomnia
note: Single-symptom (insomnia). Evidence-based CBT-I scaffolding (sleep diary, tools, learn, assessments).
elevator_pitch: Free VA / DoD / Stanford-developed companion app for clinician-delivered CBT-I; sleep diary, in-the-moment tools, validated assessments, and psychoeducation for chronic insomnia (incl. PTSD-related).
usage_adoption: Free; widely used across VA/DoD; clinician-perception study (Koffel et al., 2018) and pilot RCT support feasibility; v3 released 2023 with new features.
cbt_location: Sleep Diary; Tools (cognitive restructuring of sleep-related thoughts; relaxation; healthy sleep tips); Learn (CBT-I psychoeducation); Assessments.
cbt_structure: Companion to clinician-delivered CBT-I; not a stand-alone program.
cbt_ui_examples: Daily sleep-diary form, derived sleep-efficiency calculations, ISI weekly prompts, in-the-moment relaxation audio, sleep-thought-restructuring screens.
all_features: Daily sleep diary [CBT]; cognitive-restructuring tools [CBT]; relaxation exercises [CBT]; sleep-environment tips [CBT]; reminders; ISI / DBAS-16 assessments [CBT]; data export (xlsx) and Apple HealthKit / Google Fit integration; Learn library.
cbt_concepts: Sleep restriction, stimulus control, cognitive restructuring of sleep beliefs, relaxation, sleep hygiene, paradoxical intention.
cbt_tools: Sleep diary, relaxation audio, thought-restructuring forms.
daily_content: Daily sleep-diary completion; periodic ISI assessment.
psychoeducation: Substantial — Learn section is a CBT-I library.
standardized_measures: ISI (Insomnia Severity Index, weekly); DBAS-16 (Dysfunctional Beliefs and Attitudes about Sleep).
other_assessments: Derived sleep variables (efficiency, latency); HealthKit/Google Fit pull-ins.
cbt_degree: Core — pure CBT-I companion.

name: Cool Kids Online
stated_pop: Parents of children 7-12 with anxiety (parent-led with child)
note: Single-symptom (anxiety). Parent-led 8-lesson CBT (text/cartoon/video).
elevator_pitch: Macquarie University Emotional Health Clinic's online CBT program for parents of anxious children 7-12 (parent-delivered with Macquarie therapist accreditation/oversight).
usage_adoption: 20+ years of research support; deployed in Macquarie's Emotional Health Clinic and accredited-therapist programs; 60-75% of participants achieve anxiety-disorder-free status.
cbt_location: 8 online lessons covering CBT skills for child-anxiety: psychoeducation, detective thinking (cognitive restructuring), facing fears (graded exposure), problem-solving, parent strategies.
cbt_structure: 8 lessons over 10 weeks; parent-delivered with child; 4 brief calls with an accredited therapist for troubleshooting.
cbt_ui_examples: Lesson page with text + cartoons + parent-story videos; downloadable practice exercises; quizzes; parent-coach call schedule.
all_features: 8 CBT lessons [CBT]; detective-thinking worksheet [CBT]; fear-ladder/stepladder [CBT]; behavioural experiments [CBT]; parent-strategy modules [CBT]; therapist support calls; downloadable practice exercises [CBT].
cbt_concepts: Cognitive restructuring (Detective Thinking), graded exposure (stepladder), behavioural experiments, problem solving, parent management strategies.
cbt_tools: Online lessons, worksheets, parent-coach calls.
daily_content: Weekly lesson + daily practice exercises.
psychoeducation: Strong — parent psychoeducation throughout.
standardized_measures: SCAS / SCAS-P; ADIS in clinic studies.
other_assessments: Parent / child anxiety ratings.
cbt_degree: Core — strict child-anxiety CBT.

name: FearFighter
stated_pop: Adults with panic disorder, phobias, social anxiety
note: Single-symptom (anxiety: phobia/panic). NICE-endorsed exposure-CBT.
elevator_pitch: NICE-endorsed, SAMHSA-recognised iCBT for anxiety, panic and phobias; 9-step exposure-based self-help program with optional clinician support, deployed in NHS and US health-plan settings.
usage_adoption: 20+ years of research and development; NICE-endorsed; SAMHSA-listed; Magellan Healthcare partner deployment in the US; 41% average GAD-7 reduction.
cbt_location: 9 sequential CBT steps centred on exposure: Getting Started → How to Beat Fear → Anxiety Management → Challenging Thoughts → Core Beliefs → Exposure & SMART Goals → Exposure & Rehearsal → Continuing Exposure → Maintaining Gains.
cbt_structure: 9 steps over ~10 weeks; each ~50 min; 1 step/week recommended; optional clinician contact between steps.
cbt_ui_examples: Web-based step pages with text, video vignettes, interactive worksheets; printable end-of-step worksheets; progress emails.
all_features: 9 CBT steps [CBT]; thinking-error/cognitive-distortion identifier [CBT]; core-belief work [CBT]; SMART-goal setting [CBT]; exposure planner [CBT]; relapse-prevention plan [CBT]; printable worksheets [CBT]; email follow-ups; clinician portal.
cbt_concepts: Cognitive restructuring, anxiety management, graded exposure, behavioural experiments, core-belief work, SMART goals, relapse prevention.
cbt_tools: Online step pages, printable worksheets, exposure logs.
daily_content: Weekly step + between-step exposure homework.
psychoeducation: Strong — every step opens with focused psychoeducation.
standardized_measures: GAD-7 (primary); FearFighter outcomes scale; PHQ-9 in some deployments.
other_assessments: Exposure-task self-ratings.
cbt_degree: Core — strict exposure-based CBT.

name: Clear Fear
stated_pop: Young people 11-19 with mild-to-moderate anxiety
note: Single-symptom (anxiety). CBT framework + breathing/relaxation/mindfulness exercises for adolescents.
elevator_pitch: Free stem4-developed CBT app for 11–19-year-olds with mild-to-moderate anxiety; CBT framework with breathing/relaxation/mindfulness exercises and a personalised 'safety net' of helpful contacts and behaviours.
usage_adoption: Free; 40% increase in use post-pandemic per stem4; published evaluation (formative.jmir.org, 2025) reports 100% acceptability and statistically-significant anxiety reductions; companion app to stem4's Calm Harm (~900K downloads).
cbt_location: CBT framework underlies the whole app: change anxious thoughts (cognitive); alter anxious behaviours (behavioural); calm fear responses (physiological).
cbt_structure: Tool-first, not session-first; user opens 'Safety Net', 'Immediate Help', 'Grit Box', or 'Manage Anxiety' tools as needed.
cbt_ui_examples: Safety-net set-up flow (anxiety-reducing thoughts, coping behaviours, contacts); journaling screens; progress graphs; resource library; immediate-help SOS.
all_features: Safety Net set-up [CBT]; thought-record-style journaling [CBT]; cognitive-reappraisal exercises [CBT]; behavioural-activation activities [CBT]; breathing & relaxation [CBT]; mindfulness exercises; Immediate Help SOS; Grit Box (resilience); progress graphs; resources library.
cbt_concepts: Cognitive reappraisal, behavioural change, psychoeducation about anxiety types, relaxation, exposure (light).
cbt_tools: Journaling, safety-net builder, breathing/relaxation audio, progress graphs.
daily_content: User-driven; no enforced daily curriculum.
psychoeducation: Strong for an app of its size — explainers on different anxiety presentations and adaptive responses.
standardized_measures: Self-rated anxiety check-in; not PHQ/GAD by default.
other_assessments: Mood/anxiety progress graphs.
cbt_degree: Core — CBT is the explicit framework; mindfulness/breathing are auxiliary skills.
